qa_pairs.question: Some have claimed that Sylvester saved his companion's life by swearing. How?
qa_pairs.answer: Sylvester McCoy swore during a Doctor Who water-tank stunt to make it clear he was not acting, alerting the crew that the tank was buckling and they needed to get Sophie Aldred out.

qa_pairs.question: The company Aquamazing manufactures a roughly rectangular novelty item with an intricate brown pattern. How could it potentially answer a question that has been bugging audiences for over 25 years?
qa_pairs.answer: It was a blow-up replica of the Titanic door, intended to test whether both Rose and Jack could fit on it.

qa_pairs.question: On the 26th of November 2003, after his last flight on a particular aircraft, why was flight engineer Trevor Norcott unable to take his cap home, even if he wanted to?
qa_pairs.answer: He had deliberately wedged his cap into a Concorde cabin expansion joint, and when the aircraft cooled after landing for the last time, the gap closed and trapped it.

qa_pairs.question: In 388 BC, the boxer Eupolus of Thessaly defeated three opponents at the ancient Olympics. Funded by the men involved, several bronze statues of Zeus were erected, with inscriptions detailing the events. Why did the four men prefer not to have the statues around?
qa_pairs.answer: Because the statues were a punishment commemorating that all four men had been involved in match-fixing: Eupolus had bribed the other three boxers to lose.

qa_pairs.question: Caitlin lives in the town of Mungindi in Queensland, Australia. She recalls a time when she had to drive for a four-hour road trip to do her food shopping, yet her nearest supermarket is only five minutes away. Why?
qa_pairs.answer: During COVID, the state border between Queensland and New South Wales was closed, and although the nearest supermarket was five minutes away, it was across the border and she was not allowed to cross.

qa_pairs.question: In 2007, three young Chinese women were prevented from leaving South Korea, even though they had all the correct documentation and had not done anything wrong. Why was that?
qa_pairs.answer: They had recently had cosmetic surgery in South Korea, and their faces were so swollen and bandaged that airport staff could not verify their identities.

qa_pairs.question: In 2011, which organisation ran a full-page advert in Playbill programmes, headlined, "Our version is sliiiightly different"?
qa_pairs.answer: The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints ran the advert in programmes for The Book of Mormon.
